# Transcrição: 02-How to Set Up and Use Saved Replies Using the FB Pages App 

- **Fonte:** 02-How to Set Up and Use Saved Replies Using the FB Pages App 
- **Duração:** 05:27
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hey guys, I wanted to show you how you can use the Facebook Pages app to send saved replies to people who send a message to your Facebook page. This should save you a lot of time and this feature is currently not available in the Managed Chat Live Chat environment. So let's have a look. The only thing you need to have is the Facebook Pages app installed on your mobile device. So let's navigate to one of the Facebook pages and then let's say we're going to use the Bot Father page. In this case, select the Bot Father page and then at the bottom of the page you can see the icon for your inbox. Now when you tap on the inbox icon, you can see that the inbox by default opens with the Messenger tab, with the Messenger tab open. These are all the messages that have been sent to your Facebook page. These are the same messages as you can see in the Managed Chat Live Chat environment. What the only difference is that in the Managed Chat Live Chat environment, you're not able to create or send any saved responses yet. I'm not sure if that will come, but for now this is an awesome feature that saves me and my clients quite a lot of time. How does it work? What you need to do is you need to navigate to one of those messages. So let's say I'm going to send a message back to my friend Al. I'm going to go to Al. I was testing some stuff and he was testing some stuff. So let's say I'm going to send him a canned response back or a saved reply. The only thing I want to do is that I click on the plus icon in the bottom left corner and there I can generate, create a saved reply. In this scenario, I haven't created any saved replies yet. So what I need to do is I need to add a saved reply. I'm going to say saved reply. And then what I'm going to do is say, hi, first name. So it's not super intuitive, but the heart icon, as you can see here in the bottom right corner, the heart icon, opens up all the merge text that are currently available. So with these merge text, which are dynamic merge text, you can create all kinds of responses and be as personal as possible. So hi Al. Thanks for reaching out. Out. Please check our website. I'm going to add the website URL or call me at my phone number. And then what I'm going to do, I'm going to save this.

## [03:02 - 05:27]

And now it actually lives here as a saved reply, which I can use over and over and over again. I want to close it. And as you can see, I can now choose to send this to Al, which I'm not going to do because he's not expecting any replies from me on this one. Now I'm going to go back and if I want to create another one, I'm just going to click on the top right icon. There we go. And I can create another reply. I want to show you another case where we're using this for one of our clients, which is actually pretty cool, which is Palau Dive Adventures. And Palau Dive Adventures is a resort in Palau in the South Pacific. We sell dive packages with or without accommodation, whatever, but those dive packages are pretty expensive and people often have a lot of questions before they decide to book one of the packages we have. So the canned responses, the saved replies, save our reservations team a huge amount of time because many of the questions we get are the same. We get a lot of the same questions. So I just want to show you quickly. There was some real life scenario. I'm going to go to Palau Dive Adventures. I'm going to go to the inbox. And as you can see, we get quite a lot of people reaching out to us. And this scenario, the bot already replied to what's included. But I'm going to show you here what we have. So we have some saved replies in here. And if somebody asks us a question, let's say you reach out through the Facebook page directly and they ask us the famous question, how to get to Palau? What's the best way to get there? The team only has to select this message, click send, and then we are able to send them to a blog post we have that explains exactly how to get there. And we send them a link for the Google flights, which the team use quite often. The other question we get a lot is, when is the best time to go? Well, then we can just reply with this kind of response. Diving is a good year round, blah, blah, blah. You get the point. So I hope this all makes sense for you guys. And this will help you be more efficient with replying to people who send a message to your page. If you have any questions, drop us a line. Let us know how we can help and we'll make it work for you.
